title: Flat rate subscription with entitlements
description: Learn how to create a flat rate subscription with entitlements to manage customer feature access.

nodes.title: Create a Basic product tier
nodes.description: Create a product to represent the Basic tier with a monthly recurring price.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Create a feature for the Basic tier
nodes.description: Create a feature that will be included with the Basic product tier.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Attach the feature to the product
nodes.description: Connect the Basic feature to the Basic product so subscribers will have access to this feature.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Create a test clock
nodes.description: Run this test helper to create a test clock to control time in your test environment.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.

nodes.title: Create a customer
nodes.description: Run this test helper to create a customer for the test blueprint and associate it with the test clock. Typically, the customer is created while completing checkout.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.

nodes.title: Create a checkout session
nodes.description: Create a checkout session for the customer to subscribe to the Basic tier.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Complete the checkout process
nodes.description: The customer completes the checkout process to subscribe to the Basic tier. Preview the checkout form through the link below. Optionally fill out the form with the card number as 4242 4242 4242 4242. Use a valid future date, such as 12/34, and any three-digit CVC.
nodes.review_prompt: Open the app and confirm the user-facing flow works as described.

nodes.title: Track subscription creation
nodes.description: Listen for the webhook event to identify when a subscription is created and capture customer information.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

nodes.title: Check customer entitlements
nodes.description: Listen for the webhook event to identify when there are updates to active entitlements to provision appropriate access for the customer in your application.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

nodes.title: Advance time to billing date
nodes.description: Run this test helper to advance the test clock to the next billing cycle to see the invoice generated.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.

nodes.title: View the invoice created
nodes.description: Listen for the webhook event to identify when the invoice is created.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

nodes.title: View the invoice created
nodes.description: Use the id returned in the invoice.created event to retrieve the invoice created for the subscription.
nodes.review_prompt: Confirm the implementation calls the intended Stripe API, reuses values needed by later steps, and handles API errors without exposing secrets.

nodes.title: Wait for test clock to be ready.
nodes.description: To ensure the test clock can be deleted, check that the test clock is ready to be advanced.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the relevant Stripe CLI trigger or complete the upstream flow, then confirm the handler receives and verifies the expected event.

nodes.title: Delete the test clock
nodes.description: Run this test helper to delete the test clock to clean up test data.
nodes.review_prompt: Run the helper flow and confirm it advances test state without adding helper-only parameters to application code.
